name: ALOGIC Power Hub (Multi-Device Wireless & USB Charging Station)
gap_notes: No WiFi repeater or extender functionality whatsoever — it is purely a charging device. Retractable connectors are absent; cables must be provided separately. Only 4 devices supported simultaneously, with no whole-home network coverage angle.
pricing_summary: A$169.95 for the Wave USB-C Dock variant; Power Hub standard model retails ~A$79–A$120 via eBay AU and Techbuy
positioning_angle: Australian tech brand offering a sleek 4-device hub combining dual Qi wireless charging (10W), USB-A (7.5W), and USB-C Power Delivery (18W) in a metal-alloy/woven-fabric body designed for home or office use

name: Anker AU (Multi-Port Wireless Charging Stations)
gap_notes: Zero WiFi repeater or mesh networking features. No retractable connectors — relies on separate cables. Products are designed as standalone desktop units, not distributed throughout the home for coverage purposes. Strong brand recognition means it will be the default comparison for any charging hub launch.
pricing_summary: Anker Prime Charger 250W 6-port at A$299.95; mid-range 4-in-1 wireless stations A$69–A$149; budget multi-USB hubs from A$29
positioning_angle: Global charging leader with GaNPrime technology, up to 8-device simultaneous charging, 15W ultra-fast Qi wireless, and 67W USB-C PD ports — premium performance and broad device compatibility

name: Satechi Dock5 Multi-Device Charging Station with Wireless Charging
location: Online / Australia (sold nationally via JB Hi-Fi)
gap_notes: No WiFi repeater feature. No retractable connectors — charging cables must be purchased separately. Positioned as a stationary desk unit, not a distributed whole-home solution. Does not address dead zones or signal strength in any way.
pricing_summary: Available via JB Hi-Fi Australia; typically retails A$119–A$149 (JB Deal price may vary in-store)
positioning_angle: Premium 5-device charging station with two USB-C PD ports, two USB-A ports and a Qi wireless pad — marketed at family homes, workspaces, and classrooms as a tidy central charging hub

name: BatteryMate Australia (Wireless Fast Charging 8-Port 100W Hub)
location: Online / Australia (Australian store, ships nationally from Sydney)
gap_notes: No WiFi repeater or networking capability. No retractable connectors. Products are generic OEM hardware with basic build quality (fire-resistant ABS). Does not serve the whole-home connectivity concept at all. Very low price point creates downward pressure on any premium product entering the market.
pricing_summary: A$38.95 for the 8-port 100W wireless hub; A$34.95 for the basic 8-port dock; A$68.45 for the 20-port 90W hub
positioning_angle: Budget-focused Australian retailer offering high-port-count charging hubs (8–20 ports) with Quick Charge 3.0, Power Delivery, and Qi wireless charging built in — targeting cost-conscious households, offices, and schools

name: TP-Link Deco Mesh Wi-Fi System
location: Online / Australia (sold nationally via JB Hi-Fi, Harvey Norman, Officeworks, Bunnings)
gap_notes: No built-in device charging (wired or wireless) on any node. Requires separate power outlet and separate charger for every device. Nodes are dedicated networking hardware, not a dual-purpose charging hub. This is the closest indirect competitor for the WiFi repeater half of the product idea.
pricing_summary: Deco M4 2-pack ~A$99; Deco BE25 Wi-Fi 7 2-pack ~A$299; Deco BE65 Wi-Fi 7 2-pack A$427 (via Bunnings/JB Hi-Fi)
positioning_angle: Market-leading whole-home mesh Wi-Fi system with AI-driven seamless roaming, single network name across all nodes, and app-based management — directly addresses the dead zone elimination concept the business idea targets

name: NETGEAR Orbi WiFi Extenders & Mesh (Australia)
location: Online / Australia (sold via Harvey Norman, JB Hi-Fi, officeworks.com.au)
gap_notes: No charging capability whatsoever on any device. Must occupy a dedicated wall outlet with no dual-purpose benefit. Primarily appeals to tech-savvy or high-spend consumers; basic extenders still require manual network switching. No combined charging-and-coverage product exists in their range.
pricing_summary: Dual-band Mesh Extender Essentials Edition A$149.95; Orbi 770 Tri-Band WiFi 7 Mesh system pricing from ~A$599–A$899 for multi-pack
positioning_angle: Premium networking brand positioning mesh nodes and extenders as the definitive whole-home dead-zone solution, with Wi-Fi 7 capability supporting 200+ devices — strong Australian retail presence and brand trust

name: ALOGIC USB-C Dock Wave (All-in-One Hub with Wireless Charger & Power Bank)
gap_notes: Still no WiFi repeater functionality. Retractable connectors are absent. Primarily designed as a laptop productivity hub rather than a whole-home charging solution. The 5W wireless charging is slow by current standards. Not scalable across rooms; no network coverage intent.
positioning_angle: All-in-one portable USB-C hub combining 4K HDMI, SD card reader, 2x USB-A 3.0, USB-C PD, built-in 5W Qi wireless charger, and integrated power bank — the most feature-converged single product on the Australian market
